analysis.winningPattern: Polar (28.09% ROI, 29 trades) vs Orca v1.1 (9.49%, 737 trades) — 3x return with 25x fewer trades. The edge is selectivity, not activity.
analysis.volumeEfficiency: Polar: $60K volume / $281 PnL = $4.65 PnL per $1K volume. Orca v1.1: $302K volume / $95 PnL = $0.31 PnL per $1K volume. Polar is 15x more volume-efficient.
analysis.feeImpact: Orca v1.1 paid $269.61 in fees on $94.91 PnL — fees consumed 2.84x the profit. Polar paid $48.33 on $280.95 — fees were only 17% of profit.
analysis.frequencyCorrelation: Trades/day vs ROI: Polar 0.83->28.09%, Roach 1.63->9.42%, Orca v1.1 21.1->9.49%. Higher frequency does NOT improve returns — it erodes them via fees and poor entries.
analysis.marketContext: BTC -8.8% and ETH -9.2% over 4 days. Liquidation cascade detected. RISK_OFF regime — this is when most over-traders blow up.

recommendations.reason: BTC and ETH in clear multi-day downtrend. No 4H trend reversal signal. Protect capital until trend stabilizes.
Polar (STALKER-like, single asset, wide stops) dominates arena at 28.09%. STRIKER-like scanners (Orca v1.1) underperform despite 25x more trades.
Top 3 arena performers average <2 trades/day. Our system should target 1-3 trades/day maximum to match winning pattern.
0 trades recorded means we haven't started. When we do, enforce minimum score >=8 for all scanners. Polar-type selectivity is the proven edge.
Current RISK_OFF regime. Arena data shows top performers succeed in trending markets. Start trading only when regime returns to BASELINE or RISK_ON.
